name: Screenplay Formatting Assistance
summary:
**Conversation Overview**

The user requested that Claude reformat a script into standard screenplay format. The original content appeared to be a narrative about two characters, Jay and Frank, visiting a cemetery and then Venice Beach, where they discuss grief, dreams, and coping mechanisms. The story follows Jay's journey through loss and his search for new purpose, incorporating scenes at various locations including a dispensary, restaurant, church, and basketball court.

Claude successfully converted the material into proper screenplay format, using standard industry conventions including scene headings (EXT./INT. with location and time), character names in caps before dialogue, action lines in present tense, and proper formatting for transitions like FADE OUT. The reformatted screenplay maintained all the original story elements while organizing them into the technical structure expected for screenplays, including proper spacing, capitalization, and scene organization. Claude concluded by offering to explain or break down any aspects of the screenplay format, demonstrating readiness to provide additional clarification on the formatting choices made during the conversion process.

attachments.extracted_content:
MSNDRSTD
Written & Produced By: Jonathan T. Boyle
Directed & Produced By: Stephen Akanbi
Produced By: Zayne Amason

About the Film
MSNDRSTD is a short film inspired by the motivational self-help memoir,
“The Misunderstood Millennial” written by 4x Author Jonathan T. Boyle. This
powerful short film is a dramatical picture inspired by a true story. This story
shows the path of a former athlete who’s original dream was to pursue professional
basketball but his life crumbles after the death of his closest best friend due to
sudden diagnosed terminal cancer. While grieving from a death of a considered
loved one, navigating college life, peer pressure, identity struggles, and mental
health battles, this compelling film goes from tragedy to triumph. This film inspires
you to never trade your vision for approval and keep the faith while enduring on
your life marathon.

Film Locations
1. Angelus Rosedale Cemetery
2. Venice Beach
3. Home / Living Room
4. Fearless Church

Scene Location Themes
Short Film Duration: 10+ Minutes

Film Script
First Location: Angelus Rosedale Cemetery
Fade In
Action: Jay and Frank walking to the gravesite to mourn and place roses on the
grave after leaving the car.
Dialogue:
JAY: So, what do we do now?...
FRANK: We adjust.
JAY: It’s hard to adjust when you’re lost and in pain.
Frank: Well, what do you normally do when you feel lost?
Jay: The only thing I ever did was just hoop. So now what?
Frank: I have the perfect place to get your mind off things
Action: Walked back to the car to go to the next destination
Jay: So, where we headed now bro?
Frank: My favorite playground, Venice Beach

Fade out to next scene.
Second Location: Venice Beach
Action: Jay and Frank walking by the infamous “Venice” sign towards the
beach.
Dialogue:
JAY: So, this is your favorite playground huh?
FRANK: Yup… nothing like Venice. This is where I always come to get my head
right. The best beach in LA
JAY: What brought you out to the West Coast?
FRANK: I’m from the Motherland… grew up back in the Midwest too, but always
felt drawn to tell stories here. So, I came to Hollywood to pursue acting and my
creative dreams.
Action: Jay & Frank conversating and walking to the basketball courts. Frank
picks up a ball and then tosses it to Jay.
FRANK: You still got it right?
JAY: … Something like that… but I think that dream is over now. It’s time to find a
new one. (Shooting at the free-throw line)

FRANK: I know it’s hard to maneuver through grief, and pain especially as black
men. Keep dreaming through the pain and ask God to lead you.. You ever thought
of going to therapy. I think it will help you unpack things.
Action: Jay puts the ball down and walks away.
Dialogue:
FRANK: Ay bro… where u going?
JAY: Man forget therapy… I got a better idea.
Action: Jay walks to the dispensary and Frank follows him in.
JAY: This is my therapy… talks with the clouds…
BUDTENDER: Welcome in, what can I help you find today?
Jay: I’m looking for something uplifting… maybe a Sativa you recommend?
BUDTENDER: We have a special right now for first timers.. I got this gelato for
you.
JAY: That works .. thanks for the help!
Action: Jay gives the budtender the cash while Frank watches and then they walk
out.
Frank: Ay let me hit that bro.. I’m stressed too.
JAY: I feel you, it’s hard out here in LA at times. But this helps me cope and get
through it.
Action:: Frank and Jay smoking and leaving the Venice area

FRANK: You hungry? I got the munchies… let’s get some food around here
JAY: Man, I’m always down for food, that’s a bet.
Action: They walk into a local restaurant and get in line behind a man looking at
the menu
JAY: Ayy bro, you already order?
Action: Man in line turns around to shake their hand and greet them.
MARK: What’s good, yeah I already ordered. I’m Mark the food is bomb.
Action: Jay & Frank placing their order
Jay: We’ll take the same order he had
Action: The waiter brings out the food
Mark: So where ya’ll from?
Frank: We both grew up in Ohio in Cincinnati
Mark: Word… I’ve never been to the Cincinnati welcome to West Coast… Ya’ll go
to church out here?
Jay: Nah.. I’ve been kinda good on church right now
Mark: I feel you.. not to get all personal but are you mad at God?
Jay: I guess you can say that… I’m just confused and lost on why certain things
happen… I just lost a brother to Cancer

Mark: Sorry to hear that… Pain and grief are real, I know we just met but I run a
men’s fellowship group at this church downtown. You should slide through
sometime and I’ll show ya’ll around. You seem like good people
Frank: Yeah, I think you should go… faith has kept me strong. I’ll go with you
Action: Mark stands up and gets ready to leave
Mark: Ayy it was nice meeting ya’ll, but I gotta get headed to work, here’s my
card, just hit me up when you wanna come by.
Jay & Frank: We’ll be in touch
Action: Waiter walks by, ya’ll doing okay
Jay: Not really… let me get a drink
Fade Out
Action: Jay & Frank approach the Church doors and notice Mark at the Entrance
Mark: Glad to see ya’ll come out to the church, let me show you around..
Jay: This is like an amusement park in here.. Ya’ll got everything.
Mark: Laughs.. Yeah God is amusing.. over here we have our coffee shop, worship
center, first time area, basketball court…. And over here is our restoration center..

Frank: Restoration?
Mark: Yup.. This is where people come to get restored.. we offer family and
individual counseling… Have ya’ll ever been to therapy or thought of going.
Frank: Yeah I go to therapy and love it..
Jay: I’ve never been to therapy, always thought of it as soft..
Mark: There’s no softness with God, you can always keep it real with him. If you
ever want to take advantage of that resource let me know.. While you’re here, I
have someone I want you to meet.
Action: The faith therapist “Miss Sharee” walks by
Mark: Hey Miss Sharee! I have someone I want you to meet. This is my friend Jay.
He’s going through some things and figured he could use some guidance from
some wise counsel.
Miss Sharee: Nice to meet you Jay. I’m a faith-based therapist here in the church.
Are you free to stop by this week and chat?
Action: Jay hesitates, before responding.

Jay: Yeah… I can do that. I appreciate having someone to talk to and walk me
through unpacking things.
Miss Sharee: I’ll be here to help, just come back and see me.
Action: She shakes his hand and walks away.
Fade Out
Action: Living room scene of Jay having a mental breakdown in his living
room. He chooses between alcohol and weed to numb his pain.
B Roll: Show the train and city graffiti
Action: Jay walks back to the church to see Miss Sharee. He walks in and has a
seat with her.
Miss Sharee: Glad to see you came back, it takes a lot of courage to start therapy.
Jay: Yeah… at this point I realize I can’t do it all by myself and need help finding
my way. I’ve always felt just misunderstood and like I don’t fit in. It’s been hard to
find myself after playing sports my whole life.
Miss Sharee: What makes you feel misunderstood?
Jay: Sometimes just dealing with depression and anxiety and grief from loss… it’s
hard to unpack my feelings.. It feels like people don’t really care and I’m just in the
shadows now. I’m not sure what to do with my life now that basketball is over.
Miss Sharee: Tell me what you miss about basketball and that brought you joy.

Jay: Well, the game was everything to me. Also, a connection to my best friend
Will who died from cancer at 18. The game brought me joy but at times brings pain
because it reminds me of grief. But despite all that… playing on the court as the
point guard, it was like poetry in motion for me… worries went away.
Miss Sharee: Maybe one thing to consider is that being misunderstood is a
blessing.. God didn’t create you to fit it, but to stand out…
Action: Jay nods in agreement
Miss Sharee: Have you ever thought about journaling?
Jay: I can try… I mean I have a lot of thoughts and ideas. I’ve always been
creative.
Miss Sharee: Start with why… and everyday challenge yourself to write. Life is
like the game of basketball.. each play has a story to tell. Maybe you are being
called to tell stories off the court now… Use your pain for purpose now and inspire
others..
Jay: I feel that.. I always admired writing, and storytelling but who am I? I’m just a
young black kid, and no celebrity. Who would listen to me? What if no one listens
to the stories I want to tell?
Miss Sharee: What if they do? Every what if has a counter…
Action: Timer goes off… Miss Sharee stands up

Miss Sharee: Well, that’s all the time we have now with this session. Remember
what I told you. Never trade your vision for approval. Now is the time to re-
purpose your pain and don’t bottle it up. It will inspire the next person with the
stories God has you to tell.
Action: Jay walks away and responds
Jay: I will... Thank you.. I needed this.
B-Roll : Showing palm trees and a basketball court at a playground
Fade In:
Action: Jay walks to a basketball court and Frank is already at the court shooting
Frank: What up… I knew you would probably be here. I tried calling you but went
straight to do not disturb. Did you go see the therapist? How did that go?
Action: Jay rebounds the ball and responds
Jay: Yeah.. I saw the therapist. I was tripping at first. Almost didn’t go, but glad I
did.. I think I know what I’m called to do now. I want to tell stories off the court.
Action: Jay misses a shot and the ball rolls over to another guy at the park.
Guy at the park: Ay ya’ll tryna run next or what?
Action: Guy passes the ball over to Jay.
Jay: Nah.. I’m good bro. I got something else I gotta do now.
Frank: and what’s that?

Jay: I have stories to write now. I found a new calling.
Action: Jay shoots a shot and walks off the court smiling after he makes it.
Jay: You got next now bro.. keep shooting.
Fade out to ending showing Jay walk away with his backpack.
Ending.

chat_messages.text:
Here's the script reformatted into standard screenplay format:

```
EXT. ANGELUS ROSEDALE CEMETERY - DAY

JAY and FRANK walk from their car to a gravesite, carrying roses.

JAY
So, what do we do now?...

FRANK
We adjust.

JAY
It's hard to adjust when you're lost and in pain.

FRANK
Well, what do you normally do when you feel lost?

JAY
The only thing I ever did was just hoop. So now what?

FRANK
I have the perfect place to get your mind off things.

They walk back to the car.

JAY
So, where we headed now bro?

FRANK
My favorite playground, Venice Beach.

FADE OUT.

EXT. VENICE BEACH - DAY

Jay and Frank walk by the iconic "Venice" sign towards the beach.

JAY
So, this is your favorite playground huh?

FRANK
Yup... nothing like Venice. This is where I always come to get my head right. The best beach in LA.

JAY
What brought you out to the West Coast?

FRANK
I'm from the Motherland... grew up back in the Midwest too, but always felt drawn to tell stories here. So, I came to Hollywood to pursue acting and my creative dreams.

They approach the basketball courts. Frank picks up a ball and tosses it to Jay.

FRANK
You still got it right?

JAY
(shooting at the free-throw line)
...Something like that... but I think that dream is over now. It's time to find a new one.

FRANK
I know it's hard to maneuver through grief, and pain especially as black men. Keep dreaming through the pain and ask God to lead you.. You ever thought of going to therapy. I think it will help you unpack things.

Jay puts the ball down and walks away.

FRANK
Ay bro... where u going?

JAY
Man forget therapy... I got a better idea.

Jay walks to a nearby dispensary. Frank follows him inside.

INT. DISPENSARY - DAY

JAY
This is my therapy... talks with the clouds...

BUDTENDER
Welcome in, what can I help you find today?

JAY
I'm looking for something uplifting... maybe a Sativa you recommend?

BUDTENDER
We have a special right now for first timers.. I got this gelato for you.

JAY
That works .. thanks for the help!

Jay pays and they exit.

EXT. VENICE BEACH - DAY

Frank and Jay are smoking.

FRANK
Ay let me hit that bro.. I'm stressed too.

JAY
I feel you, it's hard out here in LA at times. But this helps me cope and get through it.

FRANK
You hungry? I got the munchies... let's get some food around here.

JAY
Man, I'm always down for food, that's a bet.

They enter a local restaurant.

INT. RESTAURANT - DAY

Jay and Frank get in line behind a man (MARK) looking at the menu.

JAY
Ayy bro, you already order?

MARK
(turning around to shake their hands)
What's good, yeah I already ordered. I'm Mark, the food is bomb.

JAY
(to cashier)
We'll take the same order he had.

The waiter brings out the food.

MARK
So where ya'll from?

FRANK
We both grew up in Ohio in Cincinnati.

MARK
Word... I've never been to Cincinnati, welcome to West Coast... Ya'll go to church out here?

JAY
Nah.. I've been kinda good on church right now.

MARK
I feel you.. not to get all personal but are you mad at God?

JAY
I guess you can say that... I'm just confused and lost on why certain things happen... I just lost a brother to Cancer.

MARK
Sorry to hear that... Pain and grief are real, I know we just met but I run a men's fellowship group at this church downtown. You should slide through sometime and I'll show ya'll around. You seem like good people.

FRANK
Yeah, I think you should go... faith has kept me strong. I'll go with you.

Mark stands up, preparing to leave.

MARK
Ayy it was nice meeting ya'll, but I gotta get headed to work, here's my card, just hit me up when you wanna come by.

JAY & FRANK
We'll be in touch.

WAITER
(passing by)
Ya'll doing okay?

JAY
Not really... let me get a drink.

FADE OUT.

EXT. CHURCH - DAY

Jay and Frank approach the church doors, noticing Mark at the entrance.

MARK
Glad to see ya'll come out to the church, let me show you around..

JAY
This is like an amusement park in here.. Ya'll got everything.

MARK
(laughing)
Yeah, God is amusing.. over here we have our coffee shop, worship center, first time area, basketball court.... And over here is our restoration center..

FRANK
Restoration?

MARK
Yup.. This is where people come to get restored.. we offer family and individual counseling... Have ya'll ever been to therapy or thought of going.

FRANK
Yeah I go to therapy and love it..

JAY
I've never been to therapy, always thought of it as soft..

MARK
There's no softness with God, you can always keep it real with him. If you ever want to take advantage of that resource let me know.. While you're here, I have someone I want you to meet.

MISS SHAREE, the faith therapist, walks by.

MARK
Hey Miss Sharee! I have someone I want you to meet. This is my friend Jay. He's going through some things and figured he could use some guidance from some wise counsel.

MISS SHAREE
Nice to meet you Jay. I'm a faith-based therapist here in the church. Are you free to stop by this week and chat?

Jay hesitates before responding.

JAY
Yeah... I can do that. I appreciate having someone to talk to and walk me through unpacking things.

MISS SHAREE
I'll be here to help, just come back and see me.

She shakes his hand and walks away.

FADE OUT.

INT. JAY'S LIVING ROOM - NIGHT

Jay is having a mental breakdown, choosing between alcohol and weed to numb his pain.

EXT. CITY STREETS - DAY

B-roll shots of trains and city graffiti.

INT. CHURCH OFFICE - DAY

Jay walks in and sits with Miss Sharee.

MISS SHAREE
Glad to see you came back, it takes a lot of courage to start therapy.

JAY
Yeah... at this point I realize I can't do it all by myself and need help finding my way. I've always felt just misunderstood and like I don't fit in. It's been hard to find myself after playing sports my whole life.

MISS SHAREE
What makes you feel misunderstood?

JAY
Sometimes just dealing with depression and anxiety and grief from loss... it's hard to unpack my feelings.. It feels like people don't really care and I'm just in the shadows now. I'm not sure what to do with my life now that basketball is over.

MISS SHAREE
Tell me what you miss about basketball and that brought you joy.

JAY
Well, the game was everything to me. Also, a connection to my best friend Will who died from cancer at 18. The game brought me joy but at times brings pain because it reminds me of grief. But despite all that... playing on the court as the point guard, it was like poetry in motion for me... worries went away.

MISS SHAREE
Maybe one thing to consider is that being misunderstood is a blessing.. God didn't create you to fit in, but to stand out...

Jay nods in agreement.

MISS SHAREE
Have you ever thought about journaling?

JAY
I can try... I mean I have a lot of thoughts and ideas. I've always been creative.

MISS SHAREE
Start with why... and everyday challenge yourself to write. Life is like the game of basketball.. each play has a story to tell. Maybe you are being called to tell stories off the court now... Use your pain for purpose now and inspire others..

JAY
I feel that.. I always admired writing, and storytelling but who am I? I'm just a young black kid, and no celebrity. Who would listen to me? What if no one listens to the stories I want to tell?

MISS SHAREE
What if they do? Every what if has a counter...

A timer goes off. Miss Sharee stands up.

MISS SHAREE
Well, that's all the time we have now with this session. Remember what I told you. Never trade your vision for approval. Now is the time to re-purpose your pain and don't bottle it up. It will inspire the next person with the stories God has you to tell.

Jay walks away.

JAY
I will... Thank you.. I needed this.

EXT. CITY - DAY

B-roll shots of palm trees and a basketball court at a playground.

EXT. BASKETBALL COURT - DAY

Jay walks to a basketball court where Frank is already shooting.

FRANK
What up... I knew you would probably be here. I tried calling you but went straight to do not disturb. Did you go see the therapist? How did that go?

Jay rebounds the ball.

JAY
Yeah.. I saw the therapist. I was tripping at first. Almost didn't go, but glad I did.. I think I know what I'm called to do now. I want to tell stories off the court.

Jay misses a shot and the ball rolls over to another guy at the park.

GUY AT THE PARK
Ay ya'll tryna run next or what?

The guy passes the ball to Jay.

JAY
Nah.. I'm good bro. I got something else I gotta do now.

FRANK
And what's that?

JAY
I have stories to write now. I found a new calling.

Jay shoots and makes the shot, then walks off the court smiling.

JAY
You got next now bro.. keep shooting.

Jay walks away with his backpack.

FADE OUT.

THE END
```

Would you like me to explain or break down any part of this screenplay format?
